mission: Implement @beep/box as a full-surface, generated, schema-first, Effect-first technical driver around box-node-sdk v10.11.1: a custom generator emits S.Class models and per-manager method wrappers from the SDK's own TypeScript types, hand-written code provides config/errors/service/streaming Layers, plus tests, docs, quality closure, PR babysitting, and Greptile 5/5 readiness.

completionGate.statement: Not achieved until this goal's work ships as a PR driven to mergeable via /yeet (bun run beep yeet: repair -> verify -> publish --pr -> monitor).

targetPackage.name: @beep/box
targetPackage.scaffoldNote: Package already exists on branch feature/beep-box-driver. Box.config.ts (Redacted token + layers) and Box.errors.ts (BoxError via CauseTaggedError) are implemented; Box.service.ts is a 'stub' marker and Box.models.ts is empty. Do NOT re-run create-package; extend the existing scaffold.

boxSdkSources.fieldCasing: camelCase (SDK deserializers map Box snake_case JSON to camelCase TS interfaces)
boxSdkSources.openEnums: Box uses open enums such as 'gsuite' | 'office_wopi' | ... | string; every object also carries rawData

methodSurface.inScopePolicy: Full non-deprecated surface: all ~305 async manager methods across all 85 managers. The SDK currently tags 0 manager methods @deprecated, so nothing is dropped on that basis; the exclusion rule still applies if a future SDK version adds @deprecated methods.
methodSurface.jsonMethodsHandling: Generated per-method wrappers grouped by manager via shared runSdkCall.
methodSurface.nonJsonMethodsHandling: Generator SKIPS methods returning ByteStream / EventStream / undefined; these are hand-written in Box.streaming.ts.
excluded: OAuth2 user-delegation auth flow (deferred; redirect flow is outside a driver's remit)
JWT App Auth config layer (deferred; large config surface)
any future @deprecated SDK manager methods (generator logs the dropped list)
methodSurface.excludedRationale: OAuth and JWT auth flows are deferred and documented; callers needing them inject a pre-built BoxClient via makeLayerFromClient. CCG and developer-token auth are first-class.

keyDecisions.decision: @beep/box lives in packages/drivers/box as a flat repo-level driver.
keyDecisions.rationale: box-node-sdk is an external SDK/service boundary, not product language or repo-owned foundation substrate.

keyDecisions.decision: A custom generator (runpod-style) parses box-node-sdk's own .d.ts and emits schema-first artifacts. Generate from the SDK types, NOT the upstream Box OpenAPI, and do NOT hand-write.
keyDecisions.rationale: The SDK exposes camelCase interfaces; decoding SDK return values requires schemas matching the SDK shape. Generating from the installed .d.ts is decode-compatible and version-locked. Hand-writing ~305 methods x 3 classes is infeasible.

keyDecisions.decision: The service exposes generated per-method wrappers grouped by manager (box.files.getFileById(payload)), each routed through one shared runSdkCall.
keyDecisions.rationale: Ergonomic, collision-free, fully typed; verbosity is free because the wiring is generated. Combines firecrawl's per-method rigor with scale.

keyDecisions.decision: Generated schemas use S.optionalKey (NOT Option), open enums as S.Union([LiteralKit(known), S.String]), and permissive decode (ignore excess props + rawData). This is a documented divergence from firecrawl's Option-wrapped hand bar and is recorded in standards/architecture/DECISIONS.md at implementation.
keyDecisions.rationale: Option on every field of 301 schemas is heavy and brittle to Box adding enum values/fields. Matches the runpod generated precedent and resolves a real scale/ergonomics tradeoff.

keyDecisions.decision: Binary/streaming managers are hand-written in Box.streaming.ts: downloads/zipDownloads -> Stream<Uint8Array>, uploads/chunkedUploads accept byte input, events.getEventStream -> finalizer-backed Effect Stream of decoded events (firecrawl watcher analog).
keyDecisions.rationale: These do not fit the JSON decode path and the generator cannot shape them; events is the event-driven surface that must preserve interruption/finalizer behavior.

keyDecisions.decision: Support a developer-token live layer (CLOUD_BOX_TOKEN -> BoxDeveloperTokenAuth), a CCG (Client Credentials Grant) layer, and makeLayerFromClient(client) escape hatch. OAuth2 and JWT App Auth are deferred and documented.
keyDecisions.rationale: Dev token covers development; CCG covers enterprise server auth (many full-surface managers are enterprise-admin). The escape hatch lets callers inject any pre-authenticated BoxClient for OAuth/JWT.

keyDecisions.decision: The generator excludes @deprecated SDK methods and logs the dropped list (no silent caps). Currently 0 manager methods are deprecated.
keyDecisions.rationale: Matches firecrawl's exclude-deprecated rule and 11-evolution-and-deprecation doctrine.

keyDecisions.decision: Generated output is split into _generated/Box.models.gen.ts (S.Class schemas) and _generated/Box.operations.gen.ts (per-manager wrappers), kept internal via package exports '\./_generated/*': null.
keyDecisions.rationale: At ~301 schemas + ~305 methods a single file is unwieldy; biome already ignores *.gen.* via root config.

keyDecisions.decision: Live integration smoke tests are read-only (e.g. users.getUserMe, folders.getFolderById('0')), env-gated on CLOUD_BOX_TOKEN, and skip cleanly when absent. No create/update/delete in CI.
keyDecisions.rationale: Avoids cost and destructive side effects, which are firecrawl-style stop conditions.

keyDecisions.decision: PR readiness requires local quality green, CI green, actionable comments resolved, and Greptile 5/5.
keyDecisions.rationale: The driver is a broad public technical boundary and must land with high confidence.

phaseStatusMeaning.completed: The phase is documented and its packet-level exit criteria are satisfied.
phaseStatusMeaning.pending: The phase is ready for an implementation agent to execute.
phaseStatusMeaning.blocked: The phase cannot proceed without a named decision, dependency, credential, or external-state change.

phases.name: Doctrine Record And Export Catalog And Local Quality Closure

qualityCommands: bunx turbo run check test lint type-test --filter=@beep/box

prClosure.packetCommit: feat: add box driver goal packet

stopConditions: box-node-sdk .d.ts shapes (recursive refs, anonymous nested types, union request bodies, DateTime) make a method or model unmodelable after reasonable generator effort.
Live integration tests would incur unexpected cost or destructive side effects.
docgen cannot process the generated surface at scale and the exclusion/inventory tradeoff needs a human decision.
A CI failure is unrelated and cannot be reproduced locally after reasonable investigation.
Greptile cannot be triggered or its score cannot be observed after documented attempts.
